data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add `purge-bridge` subcommand to the ticket CLI dispatcher that wraps purge-non-project-tickets.sh's local-only cleanup logic.

**Why**: The ticket system should manage its own bridge cleanup without callers invoking a separate script. Safety-critical subcommand: only destructive operation in the epic.

**Scope**:
- IN: New `purge-bridge` case in dispatcher routing to purge-non-project-tickets.sh logic; tracker mount AND writable check enforced before any deletion; --dry-run flag; deleted ticket IDs logged to stderr as audit record; no Jira API calls; integration test via dispatcher asserting safety checks, dry-run output, and deletion behavior
- OUT: Migrating callers (Epic e1b2-2653); removing purge-non-project-tickets.sh; Jira API integration

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `ticket purge-bridge <project-key>` is callable via the dispatcher and enforces tracker mount AND writable check before any deletion (both conditions required)
  <- Satisfies: "purge-bridge (removal of inbound bridge tickets not matching a specified project key -- replaces purge-non-project-tickets.sh)"
- When this story is complete, purge-bridge supports --dry-run flag showing which ticket IDs would be deleted without deleting, and logs deleted ticket IDs to stderr as audit record when actually deleting
  <- Satisfies: "[Reliability] purge-bridge is LOCAL-ONLY -- MUST validate tracker is mounted AND writable before any deletion"
- When this story is complete, the integration test exercises purge-bridge through the CLI dispatcher, asserts mount+writable safety check fires before deletion, verifies --dry-run produces correct output without deleting, and asserts deleted IDs are logged
  <- Satisfies: "Each new subcommand has an integration test asserting output equivalence"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Tracker mount check must verify actual tracker existence AND writability -- both checks required; false negative permits destructive ops
- [Safety] --dry-run and audit logging of deleted IDs are required
- [Maintainability] Depends on S5 (2cdd-7497) landing first; S6 (summary) depends on this story because summary traverses the link graph that purge-bridge can mutate

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
data.title: As a developer, I can purge non-project bridge tickets via ticket purge-bridge with local-only safety checks and dry-run support
